security.title: Data Security
security.subtitle: DPO Central is built with defense-in-depth across every layer. As a privacy management platform handling assessment results, incident records, vendor contracts, and data subject requests, security is a foundational requirement — not an afterthought.

categories.appSecurityDesc: Input handling, API protection, and abuse prevention
categories.dataProtectionDesc: Transport security, audit logging, minimization, and payment handling

items.auth1: Multi-provider sign-in via Google OAuth and email magic links
items.auth2: Stateless JWT sessions — no server-side session state to compromise
items.auth3: OAuth tokens are never persisted to the database
items.auth4: Account isolation — each sign-in method is kept separate
items.auth5: Cross-app SSO via secure, scoped session cookies
items.authHighlight: No passwords are stored. Authentication is delegated to trusted identity providers or cryptographically signed magic links.
items.authz1: Strict multi-tenancy — every query is scoped to your organization
items.authz2: Five-tier role hierarchy: Owner, Admin, Privacy Officer, Member, Viewer
items.authz3: Role-based enforcement on all create, update, and delete operations
items.authz4: Destructive actions restricted to Admins and Owners only
items.authz5: No raw SQL — all access via parameterized ORM queries
items.authzHighlight: All database access uses parameterized ORM queries. SQL injection is structurally prevented, not just mitigated.
items.input1: Schema validation on every API endpoint
items.input2: HTML sanitization on all public-facing inputs
items.input3: Parameterized database queries prevent SQL injection
items.input4: Validation error details hidden in production
items.api1: Timing-safe API key verification on administrative endpoints
items.api2: Payload size validation on batch operations
items.api3: Token expiry enforcement on all vendor questionnaire operations
items.api4: Public DSAR portal validates active configuration before accepting submissions
items.apiHighlight: API key comparisons use constant-time algorithms to prevent timing attacks on administrative endpoints.
items.rate1: Authentication endpoints throttled against credential stuffing
items.rate2: Checkout and billing routes protected against payment fraud
items.rate3: Public submission endpoints throttled to prevent spam
items.transport1: HSTS with 2-year duration, subdomains, and preload
items.transport2: Content Security Policy with per-request nonces
items.transport4: MIME sniffing prevention and strict referrer policy
items.transport5: Camera, microphone, and geolocation disabled by policy
items.transportHighlight: CSP nonces are generated per request, preventing inline script injection even if an attacker finds an XSS vector.
items.audit1: Comprehensive logging for all create, update, and delete operations
items.audit2: Administrative actions logged with full metadata
items.audit3: Structured production logging with no stack traces or sensitive context
items.min1: API responses return only the fields needed by the interface
items.min2: Sensitive keys are masked in administrative views
items.min3: Public email domains blocked from automatic organization membership
items.pay1: Stripe webhook signature verification via HMAC-SHA256
items.pay2: Server-side checkout prevents client-side price manipulation
items.pay3: Entitlements automatically suspended on payment failure
items.infra1: Hosted on Vercel with automatic TLS and global edge network
items.infra3: Environment secrets excluded from client bundles
items.infra4: Development-only endpoints gated by runtime environment checks

disclosure.title: Responsible Disclosure
disclosure.body: If you discover a security vulnerability, please report it responsibly. Do not open a public issue. Contact us directly at the email address listed in the repository.

errors.generic: An error occurred. Please try again.
errors.unauthorized: You are not authorized to perform this action.
errors.sessionExpired: Your session has expired. Please sign in again.
errors.networkError: Network error. Please check your connection.

generic.somethingWentWrong: Something went wrong. Please try again.

asset.activitiesUpdatedWithFlows: Activities updated — auto-generated {count, plural, one {# data flow} other {# data flows}}

activity.assetsLinkedWithFlows: Linked assets — auto-generated {count, plural, one {# data flow} other {# data flows}}
activity.flowsGenerated: {count, plural, one {Generated # new data flow} other {Generated # new data flows}}
activity.noNewFlows: No new flows to generate — the existing graph already covers this activity

assessment.autoFillAllSaved: Assessment created with all responses pre-filled
assessment.autoFillPartialSaved: Assessment created but only {saved} of {total} responses were saved

aiSystem.sentToSentinel: {count, plural, one {Sent # system to AI Sentinel} other {Sent # systems to AI Sentinel}}

quickstart.noNewRecords: All records already exist — nothing new to create

common.confidentialBody: CONFIDENTIAL — This document contains sensitive information about data protection practices. Distribution should be limited to authorised personnel and supervisory authorities upon request.

inventory.title: Data Inventory
inventory.lead: Overview of registered data assets, their classification density, and the share of personal and special-category data catalogued across the estate.
inventory.empty: No data assets have been registered.

ropa.title: Record of Processing
ropa.lead: Article 30 summary. For auditor-grade detail (systems, data elements, transfer safeguards), export the standalone ROPA report.

vendors.title: Vendors & Processors
vendors.lead: All processors and sub-processors subject to Article 28 obligations. Risk tiering and DPA status are summarised below; the full register follows by category.

ai.title: AI Governance
ai.lead: EU AI Act posture across the organisation's registered AI systems. Coverage reflects only systems explicitly catalogued — inferred or shadow AI is out of scope.

flowMap.title: Data Flow Map
flowMap.leadSingle: Production assets that participate in at least one data flow or processing activity. Nodes are colour-coded by asset type; dashed edges are auto-generated from activity–asset links, solid edges are explicitly recorded.
flowMap.leadMulti: End-to-end data flows are split across {count} cluster groups to stay legible at this scale. Each page shows one group of processing activities.
flowMap.orphansDropped: {count, plural, one {# orphaned} other {# orphaned}} (no flows, no activity link) filtered out

findings.overdueDsars: {count, plural, one {# data-subject request is} other {# data-subject requests are}} past the statutory response deadline.
findings.vendorsMissingDpa: {count, plural, one {# active vendor ({pct}%) has} other {# active vendors ({pct}%) have}} no Data Processing Agreement on file.
findings.highRiskVendors: {count, plural, one {# vendor is} other {# vendors are}} classified as high or critical risk — governance review recommended.
findings.overdueReview: {count, plural, one {# processing activity is} other {# processing activities are}} overdue for periodic review.
findings.specialCat: {count, plural, one {# special category data element is} other {# special category data elements are}} catalogued — Article 9 safeguards apply.
findings.intlTransfers: {count, plural, one {# international transfer is} other {# international transfers are}} documented across processing activities.
findings.adm: {count, plural, one {# activity involves} other {# activities involve}} automated decision-making subject to Article 22.
findings.openIncidents: {count, plural, one {# incident is} other {# incidents are}} open and under investigation.
findings.healthyProgram: No material findings detected. The privacy program is in a steady state across inventory, processing, vendors, and incident response.

ropaReport.dataFlowMapLead: Each processing activity is shown as a bordered cluster containing the assets it touches. Dashed edges were auto-generated from activity-asset links; solid edges are explicit.

assessmentReport.coverConfidential: CONFIDENTIAL — This document contains sensitive information about data protection practices. Distribution should be limited to authorised personnel and supervisory authorities upon request.
assessmentReport.dpiaCallout1: Systematic description of processing operations and purposes
assessmentReport.dpiaCallout3: Assessment of risks to rights and freedoms of data subjects
assessmentReport.dpiaCallout4: Measures envisaged to address risks and demonstrate compliance

assessmentPortfolio.coverSubtitle: DPIA, LIA & Privacy Assessment Status Report
assessmentPortfolio.coverConfidential: CONFIDENTIAL — This document summarises the organisation's privacy impact assessment program. It is intended for DPOs, privacy officers, and supervisory authorities upon request.
assessmentPortfolio.gdprCalloutBody: Controllers must carry out a DPIA before processing that is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons. The assessment must be reviewed and updated when processing operations change.
assessmentPortfolio.byStatusDesc: Current status of all assessments across the organisation
assessmentPortfolio.byRiskLevelDesc: Risk classification across all assessed activities
assessmentPortfolio.registerDesc: Complete inventory of {count} assessments with current status, risk level, completion, and linked processing activities or vendors.
assessmentPortfolio.highRiskDesc: The following assessments have been classified as high or critical risk and require priority attention, mitigation measures, and regular review.
assessmentPortfolio.mitigationDesc: Mitigation measures are tracked across all assessments to ensure identified risks are addressed with appropriate safeguards. Measures with status "Implemented" or "Verified" are counted as completed.

regulatoryLandscape.coverConfidential: CONFIDENTIAL — This document provides a regulatory compliance overview for internal use by the privacy and legal teams. It should not be distributed externally without authorization.
regulatoryLandscape.appliedFrameworksDesc: The following {count} regulatory frameworks have been identified as applicable to {org}. Deadlines shown represent the strictest requirements; where multiple jurisdictions overlap, the shortest deadline should prevail.
regulatoryLandscape.penaltyDesc: Summary of maximum penalties per applied regulatory framework. Actual penalties depend on the nature, gravity, and duration of the infringement, as well as cooperation with supervisory authorities.
regulatoryLandscape.dsarByJurisdictionDesc: Data subject access request response deadlines vary by framework
regulatoryLandscape.breachDeadlinesDesc: Maximum time to notify supervisory authorities after discovering a breach
regulatoryLandscape.transferDesc: Data transfers to countries without an adequacy decision require additional safeguards such as Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs), Binding Corporate Rules (BCRs), or approved certification mechanisms.
regulatoryLandscape.vendorFootprintDesc: Vendor operations across jurisdictions create regulatory obligations. High-risk vendors operating in multiple jurisdictions require enhanced due diligence and contractual protections.
regulatoryLandscape.consolidatedDesc: The following requirements are aggregated from all {count} applied frameworks. Requirements that appear across multiple frameworks indicate areas of regulatory convergence and should be prioritized.
regulatoryLandscape.frameworksList: Frameworks: {codes} ({count, plural, one {# framework} other {# frameworks}})

dsarPerformance.coverSubtitle: Data Subject Access Request Compliance & Metrics
dsarPerformance.coverConfidential: CONFIDENTIAL — This report contains aggregated performance metrics for data subject request handling. No individual personal data is included in this report.
dsarPerformance.privacyByDesignBody: This report contains no individual personal data. All metrics are aggregated. Completed DSAR records are automatically redacted after the configured retention period to minimize data protection risk.
dsarPerformance.byTypeDesc: Distribution of data subject requests by type of right exercised
dsarPerformance.byStatusDesc: Current status distribution of all requests
dsarPerformance.slaDesc: DSAR response deadlines vary by jurisdiction. Where multiple jurisdictions apply, the strictest deadline should prevail. Average resolution time is compared against each framework's deadline.
dsarPerformance.monthlyTrendDesc: Request volume and completion rate over the last 12 months. Monitoring trends helps anticipate resource needs and identify seasonal patterns.
dsarPerformance.agingDesc: Requests aged beyond the jurisdiction's deadline require immediate attention. Aging analysis helps prioritize workload and identify bottlenecks in the fulfillment process.

vendorRegister.directoryLead: All vendors grouped by primary category. Risk tier and DPA status are shown inline; certifications appear as chips.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.subtitle: Welcome to DPO Central — your comprehensive privacy management platform. This guide covers every feature to help you manage data protection compliance.

dashboard.title: Dashboard Overview
dashboard.description: The dashboard provides a real-time snapshot of your privacy program.
dashboard.calloutBody: Click on any stat card to navigate directly to that module's list view.

quickstart.title: Quickstart Wizard
quickstart.description: Bootstrap your entire privacy program in minutes instead of building everything from scratch.

importVendors.title: Import from Vendors
importVendors.desc: Select vendors you already use from the catalog, or import up to 5 for free from your Vendor.Watch portfolio. The wizard auto-generates data assets, data elements, processing activities, and cross-border transfers for each vendor.

industryTemplate.title: Industry Template
industryTemplate.desc: Pick your industry (E-commerce, SaaS, Healthcare, Fintech, Media, or Professional Services) and get a pre-built scaffold of assets, activities, and data flows.

quickstart.vendorWatchBody: If you have a Vendor.Watch portfolio, the quickstart wizard automatically detects your vendors and pre-selects them — no manual searching needed. Up to 5 vendors can be imported for free.
quickstart.nonDestructiveBody: The quickstart only creates new records. It never modifies or deletes existing data and automatically skips duplicates. You can run it multiple times safely.

navigation.title: Navigation
navigation.description: The main navigation bar provides access to all modules.

dashboard.label: Dashboard
dashboard.desc: Overview stats, recent activity, quick actions

dataInventory.label: Data Inventory
dataInventory.desc: Assets, data elements, processing activities, flows

dsar.label: DSAR
dsar.desc: Subject access requests, SLA tracking, public portal

assessments.label: Assessments
assessments.desc: DPIA, PIA, TIA, LIA with templates & approvals

reports.label: Reports
reports.desc: Compliance posture score, trend tracking, board reports

aiSystems.label: AI Systems
aiSystems.desc: EU AI Act register, risk classification, governance

roles.title: User Roles
roles.description: DPO Central uses a hierarchical role system. Higher roles inherit all permissions of lower roles.

rows.OWNER: Full access including organization settings, billing, and member management
rows.ADMIN: Manage all modules, users, and settings. Cannot delete the organization
rows.PRIVACY_OFFICER: Full CRUD on all privacy modules. Cannot manage organization settings
rows.MEMBER: Create and edit records, submit assessments for review
rows.VIEWER: Read-only access to all privacy data

roles.inheritanceBody: Each role inherits all permissions from the roles below it. For example, an ADMIN has all PRIVACY_OFFICER, MEMBER, and VIEWER permissions.

quickActions.title: Quick Actions
quickActions.description: The dashboard provides shortcuts to common tasks.

experts.title: Expert Help & Personas
experts.subtitle: DPO Central supports two types of users with tailored experiences for each.

personas.title: User Personas
personas.description: When you first sign in, you choose how you use the platform.

businessOwner.label: Business Owner
businessOwner.desc: For users who need privacy compliance for their own organization. You get access to the Expert Directory to connect with technical and deployment experts.

privacyProfessional.label: Privacy Professional
privacyProfessional.desc: For privacy consultants and DPOs who advise multiple organizations. You get a "My Clients" dashboard showing all your client organizations with summary statistics.

personas.tipPrefix: You can change your account type at any time from the

directory.title: Expert Directory
directory.description: Find and connect with technical and deployment experts.
directory.intro: The Expert Directory (available to Business Owners) lets you search for technical, deployment, and compliance-operations experts by specialization, country, and language.

items.search: Search by name, firm, or area of expertise
items.specialization: Filter by specialization (GDPR, DPIA, DSAR, etc.)
items.contact: Contact experts directly via their profile

clientDashboard.title: Client Dashboard
clientDashboard.description: Manage all your client organizations from one view.
clientDashboard.intro: The Client Dashboard (available to Privacy Professionals) shows all organizations you're a member of with real-time statistics:

items.dsars: Open DSARs and overdue count per client

clientDashboard.outro: Click any client card to switch to that organization's dashboard.

settings.title: Changing Your Account Type
settings.description: Switch between Business Owner and Privacy Professional at any time.
settings.bodySuffix: to view your profile and change your account type. Your navigation and available features will update immediately.

assessments.title: Assessments
assessments.subtitle: Assessments help you evaluate privacy risks before launching new projects, processing activities, or vendor relationships. DPO Central supports multiple assessment types with template-driven workflows.

templates.title: Assessment Templates
templates.description: Choose from built-in templates or create custom ones. Templates define the questions, scoring criteria, and approval workflow.

descs.CUSTOM: Build your own assessment from scratch

templates.calloutFreeBody: LIA and Custom assessments are available to all users.
templates.calloutPremiumBody: DPIA, PIA, TIA, and Vendor Risk assessments require a premium license.

creating.title: Creating Assessments
creating.description: Walk through the assessment creation workflow.

select.title: Select a template
select.description: Choose the assessment type that matches your need (LIA, DPIA, PIA, TIA, Vendor, or Custom).

fill.title: Fill in project details
fill.description: Provide the assessment name, description, related processing activities, and data assets.

answer.title: Answer assessment questions
answer.description: Work through the template questions. Each section covers a different risk domain.

score.title: Score risks
score.description: Rate the likelihood and impact of identified risks. The system calculates the overall risk level.

mitigate.title: Add mitigations
mitigate.description: For each identified risk, document mitigation measures and residual risk.

submit.title: Submit for review
submit.description: Move the assessment to PENDING_REVIEW status for approval by a Privacy Officer or Admin.

riskScoring.title: Risk Scoring
riskScoring.description: Each assessment generates a risk score based on the answers provided. Risk levels are calculated automatically.

mitigations.title: Mitigations
mitigations.description: Document risk mitigation measures for each identified risk.
mitigations.calloutBody: Link mitigations to specific risks and track their implementation status. Each mitigation should include: the measure being taken, the responsible person, the target completion date, and the expected residual risk level after implementation.

approvals.title: Approval Workflow
approvals.description: Assessments follow a structured approval workflow before they are finalized.
approvals.rejectionBody: If an assessment is rejected, it returns to DRAFT status with reviewer comments. The assessor can address the feedback and resubmit for review.

incidents.title: Incident Management
incidents.subtitle: Track and manage data breaches and security incidents from detection through resolution. The incident module helps you meet regulatory notification requirements and maintain a complete audit trail.

reporting.title: Reporting Incidents
reporting.description: Log incidents as soon as they are discovered. The system captures key details and starts tracking timelines automatically.

report.title: Report the incident
report.description: Navigate to Incidents and click 'Report Incident'. Provide a title, description, severity, and category.

assess.title: Assess severity
assess.description: Classify the severity (LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL) based on data types affected, number of subjects, and potential harm.

assign.title: Assign the response team
assign.description: Add team members to the incident and assign response tasks.

investigate.title: Investigate and contain
investigate.description: Document findings in the timeline. Update status as the incident progresses through INVESTIGATING → CONTAINED.

notify.title: Notify if required
notify.description: Determine if DPA notification is needed. If so, use the notification workflow (see below).

resolve.title: Resolve and close
resolve.description: Document root cause, remediation actions, and lessons learned. Move to RESOLVED → CLOSED.

timeline.title: Incident Timeline
timeline.description: Every incident has a chronological timeline that captures all actions, status changes, and communications.

notifications.title: DPA Notifications
notifications.description: When an incident involves a personal data breach, you may need to notify the Data Protection Authority.
notifications.warningBody: Under GDPR Article 33, you must notify your supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware of a personal data breach (unless the breach is unlikely to result in a risk to individuals' rights and freedoms). The system tracks this deadline from the moment the incident is reported.
notifications.tipBody: When you flag an incident as requiring DPA notification, the system creates a notification task with the 72-hour deadline, pre-fills the notification form with incident details, and tracks the submission status.

tasks.title: Response Tasks
tasks.description: Break down incident response into assignable tasks with deadlines.

vendors.title: Vendor Management
vendors.subtitle: Manage third-party vendors that process personal data on your behalf. Track contracts, conduct due diligence questionnaires, and monitor risk levels across your vendor portfolio.

adding.title: Adding Vendors
adding.description: Register vendors and capture key details about their data processing relationship with your organization.

navigate.title: Navigate to Vendors
navigate.description: Click 'Vendors' in the main navigation bar.

details.title: Enter vendor details
details.description: Provide the vendor name, category, description, contact information, and data processing details.

risk.title: Set risk tier
risk.description: Assign an initial risk tier (LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL) based on the type and volume of data they process.

contract.title: Upload contract
contract.description: Attach the Data Processing Agreement (DPA) or other relevant contracts.

questionnaire.title: Send questionnaire
questionnaire.description: Optionally send a due diligence questionnaire for the vendor to complete.

contracts.title: Contracts
contracts.description: Track Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) and other contracts with each vendor.
contracts.calloutBody: The system sends email notifications 90, 60, and 30 days before a contract expires, giving you time to renew or renegotiate terms.

questionnaires.title: Questionnaires
questionnaires.description: Send standardized due diligence questionnaires to vendors to assess their privacy and security practices.
questionnaires.calloutBody: You can create reusable questionnaire templates covering topics like data security, breach notification processes, subprocessor management, and data retention. Templates can be customized per vendor category.

riskReviews.title: Risk Reviews
riskReviews.description: Periodically review vendor risk levels based on questionnaire results, incident history, and contract compliance.
riskReviews.infoBody: The recommended review frequency depends on the vendor's risk tier: CRITICAL vendors quarterly, HIGH vendors semi-annually, MEDIUM vendors annually, and LOW vendors every two years.
riskReviews.notePrefix: For a formal, template-driven vendor risk assessment process, see the

dataInventory.title: Data Inventory
dataInventory.subtitle: The Data Inventory module is the foundation of your privacy program. It tracks what personal data you hold, where it flows, and how it's processed — forming the basis for your Record of Processing Activities (ROPA).

assets.title: Data Assets
assets.description: Data assets represent systems, databases, or applications that store personal data.

navigate.title: Navigate to Data Inventory
navigate.description: Click 'Data Inventory' in the main navigation bar.

add.title: Click 'Add Asset'
add.description: Use the button in the top-right to open the asset creation form.

details.title: Fill in details
details.description: Enter the asset name, type (Database, Application, Cloud Service, etc.), description, and data owner.

link.title: Link data elements
link.description: Add the personal data elements this asset stores (see Data Elements section).

save.title: Save the asset
save.description: The asset will appear in your inventory and can be linked to processing activities.

elements.title: Data Elements
elements.description: Data elements are the specific types of personal data you process (e.g., email addresses, IP addresses, health records).
elements.calloutBody: Data classified as SPECIAL_CATEGORY (Article 9 data) requires explicit consent or another specific legal basis. The system flags these elements for additional review in assessments.

activities.title: Processing Activities
activities.description: Processing activities describe how and why personal data is used. Each activity links to assets, elements, and a legal basis.
activities.calloutBody: Each processing activity must have a documented legal basis: Consent, Contract, Legal Obligation, Vital Interest, Public Task, or Legitimate Interest.

flows.title: Data Flows
flows.description: Data flows visualize how personal data moves between assets — auto-generated from your processing activity links.
flows.body1Bold: You don't have to draw the graph.
flows.body1Rest: Whenever you link a processing activity to two or more assets — via the
flows.body1Mid: button on the activity detail page, or
flows.body1End: on the asset detail page — the system automatically creates DataFlow records connecting them in a sensible order, based on the typical position of each asset type in a data pipeline (input applications first, then ingest services, then databases, then file storage, then third-party sinks).
flows.body2Start: You can re-trigger generation at any time with the
flows.body2End: button on an activity detail page. Existing flows are deduped — anything you've manually edited is preserved, and only new connections are added.
flows.tipBody: Many real systems are bidirectional (a CRM both receives and returns customer data). The quickstart vendor mappings and industry templates already mark the common bidirectional pairs, and the live graph renders the outbound and return edges as separated curves so they don't overlap.

transfers.title: Cross-Border Transfers
transfers.description: Track international data transfers and the safeguards in place for each.
transfers.warningBody: International transfers require a valid transfer mechanism. If your Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA) identifies risks, additional safeguards may be needed. See the Premium Features section for TIA assessments.
transfers.tipBody: You can export your complete Record of Processing Activities (ROPA) from the Data Inventory list view. The export includes all assets, elements, activities, legal bases, and transfer records.

dsar.title: DSAR Management
dsar.subtitle: Manage Data Subject Access Requests (DSARs) end-to-end — from intake through a public portal to task assignment, SLA tracking, and fulfillment.

creating.title: Creating Requests
creating.description: DSARs can be created manually by staff or submitted by data subjects through the public portal.
creating.typesCaption: These correspond to GDPR Articles 15-22 data subject rights.

tasks.title: Task Management
tasks.description: Each DSAR can have multiple tasks assigned to different team members.

verify.title: Verify identity
verify.description: Confirm the data subject's identity before processing. The system tracks identity verification status.

scope.title: Assess scope
scope.description: Determine which data assets and processing activities are relevant to the request.

assign.title: Assign tasks
assign.description: Create subtasks for each department or system that holds relevant data. Assign team members with deadlines.

collect.title: Collect data
collect.description: Each task assignee gathers and prepares their portion of the response.

review.title: Review and respond
review.description: Review all collected data, compile the response, and send it to the data subject.

close.title: Close the request
close.description: Mark the DSAR as completed. The system logs the completion date for compliance records.

sla.title: SLA Tracking
sla.description: DPO Central automatically tracks SLA deadlines and alerts you before they expire.
sla.warningBody: Under GDPR, you must respond to DSARs within 30 calendar days (extendable to 90 days for complex requests). The system sends email notifications at 7 days, 3 days, and 1 day before the deadline.

portal.title: Public Portal
portal.description: The DSAR public portal allows data subjects to submit requests without contacting you directly.
portal.samplePlaceholder: I would like a copy of all personal data...
portal.tipPrefix: Each organization gets a unique public portal URL (e.g.,
portal.tipSuffix: ). Share this link on your privacy policy page or website footer.

intakeConfig.title: Intake Form Configuration
intakeConfig.description: Customize which fields appear on your public DSAR portal and configure auto-acknowledgment emails.
intakeConfig.settingsDesc: Configure from Privacy > DSAR > Settings tab

quickstart.title: Quickstart Wizard
quickstart.subtitle: Bootstrap your entire privacy program in minutes. The quickstart wizard creates data assets, data elements, processing activities, data flows, and vendor records based on your industry and the tools you use.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.description: The wizard offers two complementary paths that can be used together.

importVendors.title: Import from Vendors
importVendors.desc: Select vendors from the catalog (or import from your Vendor.Watch portfolio). For each vendor, the wizard auto-generates a data asset, data elements, a processing activity, and cross-border transfer records based on the vendor's known data profile.

industryTemplate.title: Industry Template
industryTemplate.desc: Pick your industry and get a pre-built scaffold of data assets, processing activities, and data flows. Each template is designed by privacy professionals to reflect common data processing patterns for that sector.

howItWorks.nonDestructiveBody: The quickstart only creates new records. It never modifies or deletes existing data and automatically skips duplicates. You can run it multiple times safely.

vendorWatch.title: Vendor.Watch Portfolio Import
vendorWatch.description: If you have a Vendor.Watch account, the quickstart detects your portfolio automatically.
vendorWatch.intro: When you navigate to the quickstart wizard, it checks if your account has a Vendor.Watch portfolio. If found, your vendors are pre-selected and ready to import — no manual searching needed.

asset.label: Data Asset
asset.desc: One asset per vendor, typed by category (e.g. Cloud Service, Application)

elements.label: Data Elements
elements.desc: 3–10 elements per asset — identifiers, behavioral data, financial data, etc.

activity.label: Processing Activity
activity.desc: One activity per vendor with purpose, legal basis, and data subjects

transfers.label: Cross-Border Transfers
transfers.desc: Transfer records for each non-EU data location with SCCs as safeguard

vendorWatch.tipTitle: Free tier: 5 vendors from your portfolio
vendorWatch.tipBody: You can import up to 5 vendors from your Vendor.Watch portfolio for free during quickstart — no Vendor Catalog subscription needed. This matches Vendor.Watch's own free tier. To import more than 5, subscribe to the Vendor Catalog add-on.

industryTemplates.title: Industry Templates
industryTemplates.description: Each template provides a complete privacy program scaffold tailored to your sector.
industryTemplates.intro: Select your industry to auto-generate data assets (with data elements), processing activities (with legal bases), and data flows between systems. All records can be customized after creation.

ecommerce.name: E-commerce
ecommerce.description: Online retail with customer accounts, orders, payments, and marketing

saas.name: SaaS / Technology
saas.description: Software-as-a-service platform with user accounts, usage tracking, and support

healthcare.name: Healthcare
healthcare.description: Healthcare provider or health-tech with patient data, EHR, and regulatory compliance

fintech.name: Fintech
fintech.description: Financial technology with KYC, transaction processing, and regulatory reporting

media.name: Media / Publishing
media.description: Digital media, news, or content platform with subscriptions and advertising

professional_services.name: Professional Services
professional_services.description: Consulting, legal, accounting, or agency with client data and project management

afterQuickstart.title: After Quickstart
afterQuickstart.description: What to do once your privacy program is bootstrapped.
afterQuickstart.intro: The quickstart gives you a solid foundation. After running it, you should:

review.label: Review data elements
review.desc: Verify the auto-generated elements match your actual data processing and adjust retention periods

legal.label: Update legal bases
legal.desc: Confirm that the suggested legal basis for each processing activity is correct for your jurisdiction

vendors.label: Add missing vendors
vendors.desc: Register any vendors not covered by the catalog or your portfolio

assessments.label: Run assessments
assessments.desc: Use the assessments module to conduct DPIAs for high-risk processing activities

aiGovernance.title: AI Governance Register
aiGovernance.subtitle: Classify, document, and monitor all AI systems in your organization. Stay ahead of the EU AI Act and other emerging AI regulations with a centralized governance register.

overview.title: Why AI Governance?
overview.description: The EU AI Act introduces mandatory obligations for AI systems by August 2026. The AI Governance Register helps you classify, document, and monitor all AI systems in your organization.

examples.UNACCEPTABLE: Social scoring by governments, real-time mass biometric surveillance in public spaces
examples.HIGH: Recruitment and HR screening AI, credit scoring, student assessment and exam proctoring
examples.LIMITED: Customer-facing chatbots, deepfake generation tools, emotion recognition systems
examples.MINIMAL: Spam filters, AI-powered video games, inventory management systems

registration.title: Registering AI Systems
registration.description: Add AI systems to the governance register with structured metadata and risk classification.

details.title: Enter system details
details.description: Provide the AI system name, purpose, provider, model type, and deployment context. Include details about the data it processes and the decisions it influences.

classify.title: Classify risk level
classify.description: Select the risk level based on the EU AI Act classification. The system suggests a risk level based on the stated purpose and category, but you make the final determination.

document.title: Document compliance measures
document.description: Record the compliance measures in place: human oversight mechanisms, transparency disclosures, training data documentation, and accuracy monitoring.

review.title: Review and register
review.description: Review all details and register the AI system. It enters the governance register and becomes subject to ongoing compliance monitoring.

registration.tipBody: When registering an AI system, you can link it to an existing vendor in your vendor management module. This connects the AI governance record to the vendor's risk profile, contract information, and DPA status.

suggestion.title: Automatic Risk Suggestion
suggestion.description: The system analyzes the AI system's stated purpose and category to suggest an appropriate risk level.

recruitment.name: Recruitment Screening Tool
recruitment.purpose: Automated CV filtering and candidate ranking

chatbot.name: Customer Support Chatbot
chatbot.purpose: Answer FAQs and route support tickets

suggestion.infoBody: The automatic risk suggestion uses keyword matching against the AI system's purpose and category fields. It is intended as a helpful starting point, not a definitive classification. Human review is always required to confirm the risk level, as context and nuance matter significantly in EU AI Act classification.

obligations.title: Compliance Obligations
obligations.description: Each risk level carries specific compliance obligations under the EU AI Act. The register tracks which obligations apply and their fulfillment status.

risk.title: Risk Management System
risk.description: Establish and maintain a continuous risk management process

data.title: Data Governance
data.description: Ensure training, validation, and testing datasets meet quality criteria

doc.title: Technical Documentation
doc.description: Maintain detailed documentation of the system design and development

records.title: Record-Keeping
records.description: Automatically log system events for traceability and auditability

human.title: Human Oversight
human.description: Design the system to allow effective human oversight during operation

accuracy.title: Accuracy & Robustness
accuracy.description: Achieve and maintain appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity

obligations.transparencyBody: AI systems classified as LIMITED risk must clearly disclose to users that they are interacting with an AI system. This applies to chatbots, emotion recognition, and deepfake generation tools. The register tracks whether this transparency disclosure has been implemented.
obligations.deadlineBody: The EU AI Act's obligations for high-risk AI systems become enforceable in August 2026. Organizations should begin classifying and documenting their AI systems now to ensure compliance by the deadline. Non-compliance can result in fines up to 35 million EUR or 7% of global annual turnover.

linking.title: Integration with Other Modules
linking.description: AI governance records connect to other DPO Central modules for a comprehensive compliance view.

vendors.label: Vendors
vendors.desc: Link AI systems to their providers for contract tracking, DPA status, and vendor risk monitoring.

assessments.label: Assessments
assessments.desc: Trigger AI impact assessments directly from the register. Assessment results link back to the AI system record.

inventory.label: Data Inventory
inventory.desc: Map training data sources to your data inventory. Track which data assets feed into each AI system.

sentinel.title: AI Sentinel Integration
sentinel.description: DPO Central serves as the lightweight privacy-compliance register for AI systems. For deep AI governance — risk classification history, compliance frameworks, oversight gates, AI incidents, policies — use AI Sentinel.
sentinel.importDesc: When you import AI-capable vendors from the Vendor Catalog during quickstart, DPO Central automatically creates AI System records with capabilities, techniques, EU AI Act role, and risk classification from the catalog data.
sentinel.exportDesc: Send your AI System records to AI Sentinel for deep governance management. Exported systems are linked with a deep link back to AI Sentinel for seamless navigation between apps.
sentinel.configBody: The AI Sentinel integration requires the AI_SENTINEL_API_URL and AI_SENTINEL_API_KEY environment variables. When configured, you'll see the "Send to AI Sentinel" button on the AI Systems list page. Systems that have been exported show a linked badge and deep link on their detail page.

transferCompliance.title: Transfer Compliance Manager
transferCompliance.subtitle: Monitor and manage cross-border data transfers with a centralized compliance dashboard. Track adequacy decisions, SCCs, and TIA status across all your international data flows.

overview.title: Transfer Compliance Overview
overview.description: Post-Schrems II, international data transfers require careful compliance management. The Transfer Compliance Manager provides a centralized view of all cross-border data flows.

complianceStatus.title: Compliance Status
complianceStatus.description: Each transfer is assigned a compliance status based on the safeguards in place.

descriptions.COMPLIANT: Transfer has an adequacy decision or valid SCCs with a completed Transfer Impact Assessment. No action required.
descriptions.NEEDS_REVIEW: SCCs are in place but no TIA has been completed, or supplementary measures may be outdated. Review recommended.
descriptions.NON_COMPLIANT: SCCs have expired or no valid transfer mechanism is in place. Immediate action required to avoid regulatory risk.
descriptions.PENDING: Transfer has been registered but not yet assessed. Complete the compliance review to assign a status.

schremsIi.title: Schrems II Checklist
schremsIi.description: Follow the EDPB-recommended six-step framework for assessing international data transfers.

know.title: Know your transfers
know.description: Map all international data transfers in your data inventory. Identify the source, destination country, categories of data, and recipients.

tool.title: Identify transfer tool
tool.description: Determine the appropriate transfer mechanism: adequacy decision, Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs), Binding Corporate Rules (BCRs), or a derogation under Article 49.

assess.title: Assess third country law
assess.description: Evaluate whether the laws and practices of the destination country provide an essentially equivalent level of protection. Focus on surveillance powers and data access by public authorities.

supplementary.title: Apply supplementary measures
supplementary.description: If the third country assessment reveals gaps, identify and implement technical, contractual, or organizational supplementary measures to bridge the protection gap.

procedural.title: Complete procedural steps
procedural.description: Finalize required formalities: execute SCCs, obtain authorization where needed, and document your transfer impact assessment (TIA).

monitor.title: Monitor developments
monitor.description: Continuously monitor legal and political developments in destination countries. Re-evaluate transfers when circumstances change.

schremsIi.warningTitle: SCCs alone may not be sufficient
schremsIi.warningBody: The CJEU ruled in Schrems II that Standard Contractual Clauses must be supplemented with additional safeguards where the destination country's laws do not provide essentially equivalent protection. Always complete a Transfer Impact Assessment alongside your SCCs.

adequacy.title: Adequacy Decisions
adequacy.description: The European Commission has recognized certain countries as providing an adequate level of data protection. Transfers to these countries do not require additional safeguards.

supplementaryMeasures.title: Supplementary Measures
supplementaryMeasures.description: When a transfer mechanism alone does not provide sufficient protection, supplementary measures help bridge the gap.

technical.title: Technical Measures
technical.desc: End-to-end encryption, pseudonymization, key management under EU control, and strict access controls that prevent third-country authorities from accessing data in the clear.

contractual.title: Contractual Measures
contractual.desc: Additional contractual clauses requiring the importer to notify of access requests, audit rights for the exporter, and commitments to challenge disproportionate requests.

organizational.title: Organizational Measures
organizational.desc: Internal data protection policies, regular staff training, governance structures with clear accountability, and documented procedures for handling government access requests.

sccTracking.title: SCC Expiry Tracking
sccTracking.description: The Transfer Compliance Manager automatically monitors SCC expiry dates and sends alerts so you never miss a renewal deadline.
sccTracking.tipTitle: Set expiry dates when creating transfers
sccTracking.tipBody: Always enter the SCC expiry date when registering a cross-border transfer. The system sends automatic email notifications at 30 days and 7 days before expiry, giving you time to renew or renegotiate with the data importer.

regulations.title: Regulatory Tracker
regulations.subtitle: Track which privacy regulations apply to your organization, understand their requirements, and let the system automatically configure deadlines and notification windows based on your applicable jurisdictions.

catalog.title: Jurisdiction Catalog
catalog.description: Browse 40+ privacy regulations from around the world, organized by region and category.

applicability.title: Applicability Wizard
applicability.description: Answer simple questions about your organization to determine which regulations apply.

location.title: Answer location questions
location.description: Provide details about where your customers, employees, and servers are located. The wizard covers key geographic and operational factors.

identify.title: Identify applicable jurisdictions
identify.description: The wizard analyzes your answers and identifies which privacy regulations apply to your organization based on jurisdictional reach.

review.title: Review recommendations
review.description: Review the list of recommended regulations and choose which ones to apply to your organization. You can add or skip any recommendation.

configure.title: Auto-configure deadlines
configure.description: Once applied, the system automatically configures DSAR response deadlines and breach notification windows based on each regulation's requirements.

applicability.tipBody: Run the applicability wizard again whenever you expand into new markets, hire employees in new countries, or deploy infrastructure in new regions. New jurisdictions may become applicable as your footprint grows.

comprehensive.title: Comprehensive
comprehensive.desc: Full privacy frameworks covering all aspects of data protection, such as GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, LGPD, and POPIA.

sectoral.title: Sectoral
sectoral.desc: Industry-specific regulations targeting particular sectors, such as HIPAA for healthcare and PCI DSS for payment data.

ai.title: AI Governance
ai.desc: AI-specific regulations and frameworks, such as the EU AI Act, addressing algorithmic transparency and automated decision-making.

emerging.title: Emerging
emerging.desc: Newly enacted or proposed laws that are not yet fully enforced, helping you prepare for upcoming compliance requirements.

managing.title: Managing Jurisdictions
managing.intro: Once you have identified applicable regulations through the wizard or manual selection, you can manage them from the Regulations dashboard. Applying a jurisdiction creates it in your organization's database and links it to your compliance profile.
managing.infoBody: Setting a primary jurisdiction determines the default DSAR response deadline and breach notification window used across your organization. Other applied jurisdictions are tracked alongside, and the strictest deadline always takes precedence when multiple jurisdictions overlap.

impact.title: System Impact
impact.description: Applied jurisdictions directly influence how DPO Central calculates deadlines and monitors compliance across your organization.
impact.noteBody: When multiple jurisdictions apply to the same processing activity, the system follows the strictest rule. For example, if GDPR requires a 30-day DSAR response and LGPD requires 15 days, the system will enforce the 15-day deadline. This ensures you remain compliant with all applicable regulations simultaneously.

reports.title: Compliance Reports
reports.subtitle: Monitor your organization's privacy compliance posture with a single score, module-level breakdowns, and actionable risk indicators. Export board-ready reports to demonstrate accountability.

score.title: Compliance Posture Score
score.description: A weighted 0-100 score that reflects the overall health of your privacy program across all modules.
score.intro: Each module contributes a weighted percentage to the overall compliance posture score. The final score is calculated on a 0–100 scale with the following color coding:

modules.title: Module Breakdown
modules.description: Each module is scored independently based on the completeness and timeliness of your records.

inventory.example: 85% of assets have at least one activity

risk.title: Risk Indicators
risk.description: Actionable alerts highlight specific compliance gaps that need immediate attention.
risk.intro: Risk indicators surface specific issues rather than abstract scores. They tell you exactly what needs fixing and link directly to the affected records:

dsar.label: Overdue DSARs
dsar.example: e.g. "3 DSARs past their statutory deadline"

vendors.label: Unassessed high-risk vendors
vendors.example: e.g. "2 HIGH-risk vendors without a completed assessment"

contracts.label: Expired contracts
contracts.example: e.g. "1 vendor DPA expired without renewal"

ropa.label: Stale ROPA entries
ropa.example: e.g. "5 processing activities not updated in 12+ months"

incidents.label: Open critical incidents
incidents.example: e.g. "1 CRITICAL incident open for more than 48 hours"

risk.warningBody: Indicators flagged as critical (overdue DSARs, open critical incidents, expired contracts) appear at the top of the compliance dashboard with a red badge. Address these first to avoid regulatory exposure.

snapshots.title: Trend Tracking
snapshots.description: Save monthly snapshots of your compliance score to track progress over time.
snapshots.tipBody: Take a monthly snapshot of your compliance score to build a trend history for board reporting. Snapshots capture the score, module breakdown, and active risk indicators at that point in time, making it easy to demonstrate progress to management and auditors.

board.title: Board Report Export
board.description: Generate professional PDF reports for management and board presentations.

boardItems.executive: Executive summary with overall compliance posture score
boardItems.trend: Trend chart showing score history over the selected period
boardItems.indicators: Active risk indicators and recommended actions
boardItems.dsar: DSAR statistics: total received, completed on time, average resolution time
boardItems.incident: Incident summary: count by severity, notification compliance rate
boardItems.vendor: Vendor overview: risk tier distribution, contract status summary

allPdfs.inventoryDesc: — assets, elements, transfers, and a rendered Data Flow Map
allPdfs.ropaDesc: (premium) — GDPR Article 30 record with per-activity flow clusters
allPdfs.vendorDesc: — vendor list with risk tiers and contract status
allPdfs.breachDesc: — incident summaries, timelines, and DPA notifications
allPdfs.dsarDesc: — request volumes, SLA compliance, monthly trends
allPdfs.assessmentDesc: — full questionnaire with responses and risk scoring

board.flowTipBody: The Data Inventory and ROPA PDFs render a beautiful auto-laid-out diagram of how data moves between your assets, with nodes colour-coded by asset type and edges labelled with the data categories and frequency. The ROPA version groups assets into bordered clusters per processing activity. No drawing required — it's generated from your activity-asset links.
board.premiumBody: The ROPA PDF export is gated on a premium subscription. Every other PDF report is available to all users.

dpiaAutoFill.title: DPIA Auto-Fill Wizard
dpiaAutoFill.subtitle: Accelerate your Data Protection Impact Assessments by leveraging your existing data inventory. The Auto-Fill Wizard pre-populates DPIA responses so you can focus on analysis, not data entry.

overview.title: How It Works
overview.description: The DPIA Auto-Fill Wizard cross-references your data inventory to pre-populate assessment responses, turning hours of manual work into minutes.

rule.label: Rule-Based Auto-Fill
rule.desc: Maps your processing activities, data elements, and transfer records directly to DPIA questions. Every answer is traceable back to your data inventory.

ai.label: Optional AI Narrative
ai.desc: When an LLM API key is configured, the wizard can generate natural-language risk narratives and mitigation suggestions based on your data context.

select.title: Select Processing Activity
select.description: Choose the processing activity you want to assess. Optionally link a vendor to include their data processing context.

preview.title: Preview Data Context
preview.description: Review the aggregated context: linked assets, data element counts, active transfers, and associated vendors. This is the data the wizard will draw from.

review.title: Review & Edit Responses
review.description: Walk through the auto-filled responses section by section. Each answer shows a confidence badge so you know what to double-check. Edit any response before proceeding.

create.title: Create Assessment
create.description: Finalize and create a draft DPIA or LIA with all pre-filled responses. The assessment enters DRAFT status, ready for further editing or submission for review.

sources.title: What Gets Auto-Filled
sources.description: The wizard maps your data inventory to specific DPIA sections. Here is what gets populated and where the data comes from.

categories.question: What categories of data are processed?

security.question: What security measures are in place?

confidence.title: Confidence Levels
confidence.description: Each auto-filled response includes a confidence indicator so you know how much review it needs.

items.HIGH: Directly sourced from your data inventory. These values are pulled verbatim from your processing activities, data elements, or transfer records.
items.MEDIUM: Inferred from related context. For example, security measures derived from vendor certifications, or transfer risks based on destination country.
items.LOW: Template-based suggestions that provide a starting point. These are generic recommendations that should be customized to your specific context.

confidence.tipTitle: Review medium and low confidence responses
confidence.tipBody: Always review MEDIUM and LOW confidence responses before submitting your assessment. These responses may need adjustment to accurately reflect your specific processing context and organizational measures.

ai.title: Optional AI Enhancement
ai.description: Enhance auto-filled responses with AI-generated narratives and risk analysis.
ai.providersSuffix: in your environment variables to enable AI-generated risk narratives, mitigation suggestions, and plain-language summaries.
ai.fallbackBody: When no API key is configured, the wizard operates entirely with rule-based auto-fill. All core functionality works without AI — the AI layer is purely additive for generating narrative text.
ai.privacyBody: No data leaves your environment unless you explicitly configure an API key. When AI is enabled, only the minimal context needed for the specific question is sent to the provider. No data inventory is shared in bulk.

premium.title: Add-on Features
premium.subtitle: Add-on features extend DPO Central with advanced assessment types, vendor intelligence, and specialized compliance tools. Each is available for {price}/month.
premium.cardBodyPrefix: These features are available as add-ons for
premium.cardBodyMid: /month each. They are not included in the open-source core.
premium.cardSelfService: Enable any feature instantly from the billing page.
premium.cardContact: Contact our team to enable features for your organization.

dpia.title: DPIA — Data Protection Impact Assessment
dpia.description: Required by GDPR Article 35 when processing is likely to result in high risk to individuals.

whenItems.profiling: Systematic and extensive profiling with significant effects
whenItems.special: Large-scale processing of special category data (Article 9)
whenItems.monitoring: Systematic monitoring of publicly accessible areas
whenItems.tech: Using new technologies that pose high risk
whenItems.rights: Processing that prevents data subjects from exercising rights

workflowItems.describe: Describe the processing operations and purposes
workflowItems.identify: Identify and assess risks to individuals
workflowItems.document: Document the assessment and seek DPO consultation
workflowItems.consult: If residual risk is high, consult with the supervisory authority

dpia.infoSelfPrefix: Available as an add-on. Visit the
dpia.infoContactSuffix: to enable DPIA assessments for your organization.

pia.title: PIA — Privacy Impact Assessment
pia.description: A broader privacy assessment framework for evaluating privacy implications beyond GDPR-specific requirements.
pia.diffTitle: How does PIA differ from DPIA?

diffItems.scopeBody: PIAs cover privacy concerns beyond GDPR, including CCPA, LGPD, and other frameworks
diffItems.orgBody: Evaluates privacy risk to both individuals and the organization
diffItems.flexBody: Adaptable to different jurisdictions and regulatory requirements
diffItems.casesBody: New product launches, marketing campaigns, AI/ML systems, M&A due diligence

pia.infoSelfPrefix: Available as an add-on. Visit the
pia.infoContactSuffix: to enable PIA assessments for your organization.

tia.title: TIA — Transfer Impact Assessment
tia.description: Evaluate the risks of international data transfers following the Schrems II ruling.

evalItems.legal: The legal framework of the recipient country
evalItems.supplementary: Whether supplementary measures are needed beyond SCCs/BCRs

tia.infoSelfPrefix: Available as an add-on. Visit the
tia.infoContactSuffix: to enable TIA assessments for your organization.

vendorRisk.title: Vendor Risk Assessment
vendorRisk.description: A specialized assessment template designed for evaluating vendor-specific privacy and security risks.

coversItems.incident: Incident response and breach notification capabilities

vendorRisk.tipBody: Use Vendor Risk Assessments alongside vendor questionnaires for a complete picture. The assessment provides the formal risk evaluation, while questionnaires gather detailed evidence from the vendor.
vendorRisk.infoSelfPrefix: Available as an add-on. Visit the

vendorCatalog.title: Vendor Catalog
vendorCatalog.description: Search and import from a starter catalog of common MarTech, AI, and SaaS processors. Entries are a starting point for your own assessment, not an audit.
vendorCatalog.cardSubtitle: Search a starter catalog of common processors
vendorCatalog.infoSelfPrefix: Available as an add-on. Visit the
vendorCatalog.infoContactSuffix: to enable the Vendor Catalog for your organization.

publicOverview.metaDescription: DPO Central documentation — quick start guide, user roles, and module overview for data inventory, DSAR, assessments, incidents, and vendor management.
publicOverview.ogDescription: Quick start guide, user roles, and module overview for GDPR privacy management.
publicOverview.heroSubtitle: A single source of truth for your privacy management program. DPO Central helps organizations manage data inventory, DSARs, assessments, incidents, and vendor relationships from one unified platform.

step1.label: Sign in
step1.desc: Use your email magic link or Google account

step2.label: Set up your organization
step2.desc: Create or join an organization and invite your team

step3.label: Build your data inventory
step3.desc: Register data assets, elements, and processing activities

step4.label: Start managing privacy
step4.desc: Handle DSARs, run assessments, and track incidents

roles.title: User Roles
roles.intro: DPO Central uses role-based access control. Each role inherits permissions from the roles below it.

Owner.desc: Full control including billing, team management, and organization settings

Admin.desc: Manage users, configure modules, and access all privacy features

PrivacyOfficer.desc: Full access to all privacy modules, run assessments, manage incidents

Member.desc: Create and edit records, submit DSARs, report incidents

Viewer.desc: Read-only access to dashboards, reports, and records

inventory.title: Data Inventory
inventory.description: Track data assets, elements, processing activities, and cross-border transfers. Generate your Record of Processing Activities (ROPA) automatically.

dsar.title: DSAR Management
dsar.description: Handle data subject access requests end-to-end with SLA tracking, task management, and a public intake portal.

assessments.title: Assessments
assessments.description: Conduct DPIAs, PIAs, LIAs, TIAs, and vendor risk assessments using configurable templates with approval workflows.

incidents.title: Incident Management
incidents.description: Track privacy incidents, manage response timelines, coordinate DPA notifications, and assign response tasks.

vendors.title: Vendor Management
vendors.description: Maintain your vendor register, track contracts, send questionnaires, and monitor third-party risk.

reports.title: Reports & PDF Exports
reports.intro: Export professionally formatted PDF reports across every module. Designed for regulators, auditors, board presentations, and internal governance reviews.

assessmentExport.name: Assessment Export
assessmentExport.desc: Individual DPIA/LIA detail with Q&A, risk scores, mitigations

portfolio.name: Assessment Portfolio
portfolio.desc: Cross-assessment program status by type, risk, and completion

dsar.name: DSAR Performance
dsar.desc: Request volumes, SLA compliance, resolution trends (no PII)

regulatory.name: Regulatory Landscape
regulatory.desc: Jurisdiction analysis with transfer exposure and penalty summary

inventory.name: Data Inventory
inventory.desc: Asset register with data elements, flows, and transfers

ropa.name: ROPA
ropa.desc: Record of Processing Activities per GDPR Article 30

vendor.name: Vendor Register
vendor.desc: Third-party inventory with risk tiers, contracts, and certifications

breach.name: Breach Register
breach.desc: Incident history with notifications, timelines, and severity breakdown

licensing.title: Open Core Licensing
licensing.intro: DPO Central follows an open core model. The core platform is available under AGPL-3.0, while advanced features require a commercial license.

premiumItems.catalog: Vendor Catalog (starter catalog of common processors)

publicDataInventory.metaDescription: Track data assets, elements, processing activities, and cross-border transfers. Generate your GDPR Record of Processing Activities (ROPA) automatically.
publicDataInventory.ogDescription: Map your data landscape — assets, elements, processing activities, data flows, and cross-border transfers with automatic ROPA generation.
publicDataInventory.title: Data Inventory
publicDataInventory.subtitle: Map your organization's data landscape. Track every asset, data element, processing activity, and cross-border transfer to maintain a complete Record of Processing Activities (ROPA).

assets.title: Data Assets
assets.intro: Data assets represent the systems, applications, and databases that store or process personal data. Each asset records its type, owner, associated data elements, and processing activities.

DATABASE.desc: Relational or NoSQL databases storing personal data

APPLICATION.desc: Software applications that process user data

FILE_SYSTEM.desc: File storage systems containing personal records

CLOUD_SERVICE.desc: Third-party SaaS tools processing personal data

PHYSICAL.desc: Physical storage of personal data documents

elements.title: Data Elements
elements.intro: Data elements describe the specific categories of personal data your organization processes. Each element has a sensitivity classification ranging from Public to Special Category.
elements.examples: Examples include full name, email address, IP address, health records, biometric data, and financial information. Link elements to assets to understand exactly where each type of personal data resides.

activities.title: Processing Activities
activities.intro: Document every processing activity with its legal basis, purpose, data categories, and retention period. This forms the core of your ROPA.
activities.outro: Each activity records the controller, processor, categories of data subjects, and whether automated decision-making is involved. Activities link to data assets and elements for full traceability.

flows.title: Data Flows
flows.intro: Visualize how personal data moves through your organization using the interactive data flow diagram. Map source-to-destination transfers between assets, departments, and third parties.

collection.label: Collection
collection.description: User submits data via web form

transfers.title: Cross-Border Transfers
transfers.intro: Track international data transfers with their legal mechanisms. Document Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs), adequacy decisions, Binding Corporate Rules (BCRs), and other safeguards.

howTo.title: How to Add a Data Asset

navigate.title: Navigate to Data Inventory
navigate.description: Open the Data Inventory module from the main navigation.

click.title: Click Add Asset
click.description: Click the 'Add Asset' button in the top right corner of the assets list.

fill.title: Fill in Asset Details
fill.description: Enter the asset name, type, owner, description, and associated data elements.
fill.detail1: Select the asset type (Database, Application, Cloud Service, etc.)
fill.detail2: Assign a data owner from your organization members
fill.detail3: Link relevant data elements to this asset

link.title: Link Processing Activities
link.description: Associate the asset with its processing activities and legal bases.

save.title: Review and Save
save.description: Review all details and save. The asset will appear in your data inventory and data flow diagram.

exports.title: PDF Exports
exports.intro: Export your data inventory and ROPA as professionally formatted PDFs for regulators, auditors, or internal governance reviews.
exports.inventoryDesc: Complete asset inventory with data elements per asset (category, sensitivity, retention), data flows between systems, and international transfer details with safeguard mechanisms.
exports.ropaDesc: Record of Processing Activities per GDPR Article 30 — processing purpose, legal basis, data subjects, recipients, retention, linked systems, and international transfers per activity.

publicDsar.metaDescription: Handle data subject access requests end-to-end with SLA tracking, task management, privacy-by-design data handling, and a public intake portal. Supports all GDPR rights (Art. 15-21).
publicDsar.ogDescription: End-to-end DSAR lifecycle — privacy-by-design intake, identity verification, SLA tracking, auto-redaction, and performance reporting.
publicDsar.title: DSAR Management
publicDsar.subtitle: Handle data subject access requests from submission to completion. Track SLA deadlines, assign tasks to your team, and provide a public portal for data subjects to submit requests — all with privacy-by-design data handling and automatic PII redaction.

lifecycle.title: Request Lifecycle
lifecycle.intro: Every DSAR moves through a defined lifecycle with automatic SLA tracking. The system monitors deadlines and alerts your team when action is needed.

privacy.title: Privacy by Design
privacy.intro: DPO Central is designed so that facilitating DSARs does not create new privacy risks. We collect the minimum data needed, redact it automatically after the retention period, and never include individual PII in reports.

min.title: Data Minimization
min.desc: The intake form collects only name, email, and request type. Phone and relationship are optional. Address is never collected via the portal.

consent.title: Consent at Intake
consent.desc: Data subjects must explicitly consent to processing before submitting. The privacy notice explains retention periods and redaction.

auto.title: Auto-Redaction
auto.desc: After the retention period (default 90 days post-completion), all PII is automatically replaced with 'REDACTED'. The anonymized audit trail is preserved.

pii.title: PII-Free Reports
pii.desc: The DSAR Performance Report contains only aggregated metrics — request volumes, SLA rates, type distributions. Zero individual data.

manual.title: Manual Redaction & Deletion
manual.desc: Admins can manually redact PII or hard-delete completed requests at any time, without waiting for the retention period.

audit.title: Audit Trail Integrity
audit.desc: Actions, timestamps, and staff IDs are preserved after redaction. Who did what and when remains traceable — only the data subject's PII is removed.

types.title: Request Types
types.intro: DPO Central supports all GDPR data subject rights as request types.

items.ACCESS: Right to access personal data held by the organization (Art. 15)
items.ERASURE: Right to be forgotten — deletion of personal data (Art. 17)
items.RECTIFICATION: Right to correct inaccurate personal data (Art. 16)
items.PORTABILITY: Right to receive data in a machine-readable format (Art. 20)
items.OBJECTION: Right to object to processing of personal data (Art. 21)
items.RESTRICTION: Right to restrict processing of personal data (Art. 18)

portal.title: Public Portal
portal.intro: Give data subjects a dedicated portal to submit requests. The portal is customizable with your organization's branding and generates a shareable link you can add to your privacy policy.
portal.urlExplain: Share this URL in your privacy policy so data subjects can submit requests directly. The portal includes a consent checkbox and privacy notice explaining how their data will be handled.

tasks.title: Task Management
tasks.intro: Break down each DSAR into actionable tasks and assign them to team members. Track progress, add notes, and ensure nothing falls through the cracks.
tasks.exampleLabel: Example Tasks for an Access Request

sla.title: SLA Tracking
sla.intro: The system automatically calculates SLA deadlines based on your applied jurisdictions. Deadlines are per-framework — GDPR (30 days), CCPA/CPRA (45 days), LGPD (15 days), and 40+ others.

retention.title: Data Retention & Auto-Redaction
retention.intro: DPO Central automatically redacts personal data from completed DSAR records after the configured retention period. This ensures the platform does not become a risk vector for the organizations it serves.

reporting.title: Performance Report (PDF Export)
reporting.intro: Export a DSAR Performance Report as a polished PDF for regulators, auditors, or board presentations. The report contains only aggregated metrics — no individual personal data.

exec.detail: Total requests, on-time rate, avg resolution

receive.title: Receive Request
receive.description: A data subject submits a request through the public portal (with consent) or you create one manually in the dashboard.

verify.title: Verify Identity
verify.description: Confirm the identity of the data subject. Update the request status to reflect the verification outcome.
verify.detail1: Request additional ID documents if needed
verify.detail2: Mark identity as verified or rejected

assign.title: Create and Assign Tasks
assign.description: Break the request into tasks and assign them to the relevant team members.

collect.title: Collect and Review Data
collect.description: Team members search systems, compile data, and upload findings. The privacy officer reviews for completeness.

deliver.title: Deliver Response
deliver.description: Send the final response to the data subject and mark the request as completed. PII will be auto-redacted after the retention period.

publicAssessments.metaDescription: Conduct DPIAs, PIAs, TIAs, LIAs, and vendor risk assessments with configurable templates, approval workflows, and risk scoring.
publicAssessments.ogDescription: Privacy impact assessments — DPIA, PIA, TIA, LIA, and vendor risk with templates, approval workflows, and risk scoring.
publicAssessments.title: Assessments
publicAssessments.subtitle: Conduct privacy impact assessments using configurable templates. Score risks, document mitigations, and manage approval workflows for DPIAs, PIAs, LIAs, TIAs, and vendor risk assessments.

templates.title: Assessment Templates
templates.intro: Choose from built-in templates or create custom assessments. Each template includes pre-configured questions, risk criteria, and approval workflows.

LIA.name: Legitimate Interests Assessment
LIA.desc: Evaluate whether legitimate interests can be relied upon as a legal basis

CUSTOM.name: Custom Assessment
CUSTOM.desc: Create your own assessment template with custom questions and criteria

DPIA.name: Data Protection Impact Assessment
DPIA.desc: Required under GDPR Article 35 for high-risk processing activities

PIA.name: Privacy Impact Assessment
PIA.desc: Broader privacy analysis for new projects and systems

TIA.name: Transfer Impact Assessment
TIA.desc: Evaluate safeguards for international data transfers (Schrems II)

VENDOR.name: Vendor Risk Assessment
VENDOR.desc: Assess privacy risks of third-party vendors and processors

approvals.title: Approval Workflow
approvals.intro: Assessments move through a structured approval workflow. Each stage has clear ownership and the system tracks who approved what and when.

riskScoring.title: Risk Scoring
riskScoring.intro: Each assessment calculates an overall risk level based on the likelihood and impact of identified risks. The system supports four risk levels.
riskScoring.outro: Risk scores are calculated from individual question responses and can be overridden by the assessor with justification. The overall risk level drives review requirements and mitigation priorities.

mitigations.title: Risk Mitigations
mitigations.intro: Document mitigation measures for each identified risk. Track implementation status and assign ownership for follow-up actions.

items.encryption: Implement data encryption at rest and in transit
items.access: Add access controls and audit logging

select.title: Select Template
select.description: Choose an assessment template (LIA, DPIA, PIA, TIA, Vendor, or Custom) from the Assessments module.

scope.title: Set Scope and Context
scope.description: Define the assessment scope, processing activity being evaluated, and relevant data assets.
scope.detail1: Link to specific processing activities from your data inventory
scope.detail2: Identify the data elements and data subjects involved

complete.title: Complete Questions
complete.description: Answer each question in the template. The system calculates risk scores as you progress.

mitigations.title: Document Mitigations
mitigations.description: For each identified risk, document mitigation measures, assign owners, and set deadlines.

submit.title: Submit for Review
submit.description: Submit the completed assessment for approval. Reviewers can approve, reject, or request changes.

approve.title: Final Approval
approve.description: Once approved, the assessment is locked and stored as a compliance record.

exports.title: PDF Exports
exports.intro: Export individual assessments or your entire assessment portfolio as professionally formatted PDF reports. Designed for regulators, auditors, and board presentations.
exports.individualDesc: Export any single assessment as a detailed PDF with cover page, executive summary, all questions and responses, risk scores, mitigations, and approval history.

individualFeatures.cover: Cover page with GDPR Art. 35 reference
individualFeatures.qa: Section-by-section Q&A with inline risk badges

exports.portfolioDesc: Export a cross-assessment summary showing your entire impact assessment program status — by type, risk level, and completion.

portfolioFeatures.status: Status & risk distribution across all assessments

publicIncidents.metaDescription: Track privacy incidents from detection to resolution. Manage severity classification, 72-hour DPA notification (Art. 33 GDPR), response tasks, and incident timelines.
publicIncidents.ogDescription: Breach tracking, DPA notification (Art. 33), severity classification, response tasks, and full incident timeline.
publicIncidents.title: Incident Management
publicIncidents.subtitle: Track privacy incidents from initial report through resolution. Manage response timelines, coordinate DPA notifications, and maintain a complete audit trail of every action taken.

lifecycle.title: Incident Lifecycle
lifecycle.intro: Every incident follows a defined lifecycle. The system tracks status transitions and timestamps every change for compliance evidence.

severity.title: Severity Levels
severity.intro: Classify incidents by severity to prioritize response efforts and determine notification obligations.

items.LOW: Minor incident, no personal data at risk
items.HIGH: Significant data exposure, DPA notification likely
items.CRITICAL: Large-scale breach, immediate DPA notification required

notifications.title: DPA Notification
notifications.intro: Under GDPR Article 33, personal data breaches must be reported to the supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware of the breach. DPO Central tracks this deadline automatically.
notifications.ruleBody: When a breach is likely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of individuals, notify the supervisory authority without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after becoming aware of it. The system tracks this deadline from the moment the incident is reported and displays countdown timers on your dashboard.

dashboard.title: Incident Dashboard
dashboard.intro: The incidents dashboard gives you an at-a-glance view of your organization's incident status.

timeline.title: Incident Timeline
timeline.intro: Every incident maintains a detailed timeline recording all events, status changes, communications, and actions. This provides the audit trail needed for regulatory compliance.

entries.rootCause: Root cause identified: misconfigured access control

tasks.title: Response Tasks
tasks.intro: Assign response tasks to team members to coordinate the incident response. Track completion and ensure all remediation steps are carried out.

report.title: Report the Incident
