report.description: Navigate to the Incidents module and click 'Report Incident'. Provide initial details: title, description, severity, and affected systems.

triage.title: Triage and Investigate
triage.description: The privacy officer reviews the report, assigns a severity level, and begins the investigation.
triage.detail1: Determine scope: what data was affected and how many subjects
triage.detail2: Identify root cause and attack vector if applicable
triage.detail3: Assess whether DPA notification is required

contain.title: Contain and Mitigate
contain.description: Take immediate action to contain the incident and prevent further data loss.

notify.title: Notify Authorities
notify.description: If required, submit DPA notification within 72 hours. Notify affected data subjects if the breach poses a high risk to their rights.

resolve.title: Resolve and Document
resolve.description: Once the incident is fully resolved, document lessons learned, update policies, and close the incident record.

exports.title: PDF Exports
exports.intro: Export your breach register as a formatted PDF for regulatory notifications, internal reviews, or board reporting.
exports.registerDesc: Full incident inventory with severity breakdown, notification status per jurisdiction, containment actions, root cause analysis, lessons learned, and per-incident timelines. Includes summary statistics for total incidents, critical count, and pending notifications.

publicVendors.metaDescription: Maintain your vendor register with contract tracking, privacy questionnaires, risk tiers, and periodic review workflows.
publicVendors.ogDescription: Vendor register, contract management, privacy questionnaires, risk tier classification, and review workflows.
publicVendors.title: Vendor Management
publicVendors.subtitle: Maintain a complete register of third-party vendors and processors. Track contracts, send privacy questionnaires, monitor risk tiers, and ensure your supply chain meets your privacy standards.

register.title: Vendor Register
register.intro: Your vendor register is the central record of all third parties that process personal data on your behalf. Each vendor entry captures contact details, processing purposes, contract status, and risk tier.

ACTIVE.label: ACTIVE
ACTIVE.desc: Vendor is approved and actively processing data

UNDER_REVIEW.label: UNDER REVIEW
UNDER_REVIEW.desc: Vendor is being evaluated or re-assessed

OFFBOARDED.label: OFFBOARDED
OFFBOARDED.desc: Vendor relationship terminated, data return/deletion confirmed

riskTiers.title: Risk Tiers
riskTiers.intro: Vendors are classified into risk tiers based on the volume and sensitivity of data they process, their security posture, and geographic considerations.
riskTiers.outro: Higher-risk vendors require more frequent reviews, stronger contractual safeguards, and may trigger a vendor risk assessment.

dashboard.title: Vendor Dashboard
dashboard.intro: Get an overview of your vendor landscape at a glance.

contracts.title: Contract Management
contracts.intro: Track Data Processing Agreements (DPAs), Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs), and other contractual documents for each vendor. Set renewal reminders and monitor expiration dates.
contracts.label: Key Contract Documents

dpa.purpose: Defines processing terms under GDPR Article 28

sub.purpose: Terms for the vendor's own sub-processors

questionnaires.title: Privacy Questionnaires
questionnaires.intro: Send privacy and security questionnaires to vendors to assess their data protection practices. Track responses and flag areas of concern for follow-up.
questionnaires.label: Questionnaire Topics

review.title: Vendor Review Process
review.intro: The vendor review lifecycle from onboarding to ongoing monitoring.

add.title: Add Vendor to Register
add.description: Navigate to the Vendor Management module and click 'Add Vendor'. Enter the vendor name, contact details, and processing purpose.

send.title: Send Questionnaire
send.description: Select a privacy questionnaire template and send it to the vendor contact. The system tracks response status and deadlines.

review.title: Review Responses
review.description: Evaluate the vendor's questionnaire responses. Flag any areas of concern and assign a preliminary risk tier.

execute.title: Execute Contracts
execute.description: Upload and track the Data Processing Agreement (DPA) and any additional contractual documents.

approve.title: Approve and Monitor
approve.description: Approve the vendor for active use. Set up periodic review reminders based on the vendor's risk tier.

exports.title: PDF Exports
exports.intro: Export your vendor register as a formatted PDF for audits, due diligence reviews, or regulatory submissions.
exports.registerDesc: Complete vendor inventory with risk tiers, certifications, data categories processed, countries of operation, contract/DPA status, and review schedules. Includes summary statistics and per-vendor detail cards.

lia3.title: Balancing Test
lia3.description: Balance interests against data subject rights

lia4.title: Safeguards
lia4.description: Document safeguards to protect data subjects

lia1_1.text: What is the legitimate interest being pursued?
lia1_1.helpText: Describe the specific interest, not just 'business purposes'

lia1_2.text: Is this interest recognized as legitimate under law?

lia1_3.text: What benefit does pursuing this interest provide?

lia2_1.text: Is the processing necessary to achieve the interest?

lia2_2.text: Are there less intrusive ways to achieve the same goal?

lia3_1.text: What is the nature of the data being processed?

lia3_2.text: Would data subjects reasonably expect this processing?

lia3_3.text: What is the potential impact on data subjects?

lia4_1.text: What safeguards are in place to protect data subjects?

lia4_2.text: Have you provided clear privacy information about this processing?

custom1.title: Overview
custom1.description: Describe the scope and purpose of this assessment

custom1_1.text: What is the purpose of this assessment?
custom1_1.helpText: Describe what you are evaluating and why

custom1_2.text: What is the scope of this assessment?
custom1_2.helpText: Define the systems, processes, or data flows being assessed

custom2_1.text: What are the key risks identified?

custom2_2.text: What is the overall risk level?

custom2_3.text: Are there any legal or regulatory concerns?

custom3_1.text: What mitigations are in place or recommended?

tia1.title: Transfer Description
tia1.description: Describe the data transfer, including the parties involved, the data categories, and the transfer mechanism

tia2.title: Destination Country Legal Framework
tia2.description: Assess whether the laws and practices of the destination country provide an essentially equivalent level of protection to the EU/EEA

tia3.title: Supplementary Measures
tia3.description: Identify technical, contractual, and organisational measures that supplement the transfer mechanism to ensure an equivalent level of protection

tia4.title: Risk Assessment & Conclusion
tia4.description: Weigh the residual risk after supplementary measures and determine whether the transfer can proceed

tia5.title: Ongoing Monitoring
tia5.description: Define how the transfer will be monitored and when this assessment should be reviewed

tia1_1.text: What personal data is being transferred?
tia1_1.helpText: Describe the categories of personal data (e.g., contact details, behavioural data, special categories) and estimated volume

tia1_2.text: Who is the data exporter and who is the data importer?
tia1_2.helpText: Name the organisations, their roles (controller/processor), and the countries they are established in

tia1_3.text: What is the transfer mechanism (Article 46 GDPR)?

tia1_4.text: What is the purpose of the transfer?

tia1_5.text: Is the transfer onward-transferred to a further third country?

tia2_1.text: Does the destination country have an EU adequacy decision?

tia2_2.text: Does the destination country have legislation permitting government access to personal data (e.g., surveillance, national security)?
options: Legislation exists but with effective oversight and safeguards
Broad surveillance powers with limited oversight
Mass surveillance or no independent judicial oversight

tia2_3.text: Describe the relevant laws and government access practices in the destination country
tia2_3.helpText: Reference specific legislation (e.g., FISA Section 702, UK IPA 2016, CLOUD Act). Include publicly available government access reports and transparency data where possible.

tia2_4.text: Is the data importer subject to government access requests in practice?
options: No — data importer has never received a government access request
Possible — data importer operates in a sector that may be targeted
Yes — data importer publishes transparency reports confirming access requests

tia2_5.text: Are effective legal remedies available to EU data subjects in the destination country?
options: Partial — administrative remedies but no judicial review

tia3_1.text: What technical measures are in place?
tia3_1.helpText: Select all technical measures that apply. Effective encryption with EU-held keys is the strongest technical safeguard.
options: Encryption at rest (importer cannot access keys)
Split processing (no single party sees full dataset)

tia3_2.text: What contractual measures supplement the SCCs or transfer agreement?
options: Obligation to challenge government access requests
Obligation to notify exporter of government access (where legally permitted)
Prohibition on onward transfers without prior authorisation
Audit rights for the data exporter
Data localisation obligation (processing only in specified countries)
Suspension / termination clause if legal framework changes

tia3_3.text: What organisational measures are in place?
options: Internal policies restricting government access response procedures
Staff training on data protection obligations
Designated privacy / compliance team at the importer
Regular compliance audits or certifications (e.g., ISO 27001, SOC 2)
Incident response plan covering government access scenarios

tia3_4.text: Are the supplementary measures effective at preventing or mitigating the identified risks?
tia3_4.helpText: Explain why the combination of measures above is sufficient — or insufficient — to ensure essentially equivalent protection. If the data must be available in the clear to the importer (e.g., for processing), encryption alone may not be effective against government access orders.

tia4_1.text: What is the likelihood that public authorities in the destination country will access the transferred data?
options: Negligible — sector/data type not targeted, strong safeguards
Low — possible but unlikely given safeguards and data type
Medium — sector is known to be targeted; safeguards may not fully prevent access
High — evidence of routine access to this type of data/sector

tia4_2.text: What would be the severity of impact on data subjects if their data were accessed?
options: Negligible — pseudonymised or non-sensitive data, minimal harm
Low — limited personal data, low sensitivity
Medium — identifiable personal data, moderate sensitivity
High — special category data, vulnerable individuals, or potential for significant harm

tia4_3.text: Overall conclusion: can the transfer proceed?
options: Yes — supplementary measures provide essentially equivalent protection
Yes, with conditions — additional measures required before transfer can proceed
No — residual risk is too high; transfer should be suspended or alternative mechanism sought

tia4_4.text: If additional conditions are required, describe them
tia4_4.helpText: List specific actions that must be completed before the transfer can proceed (e.g., implement EU-held encryption keys, switch to a different processor).

tia5_1.text: How will changes in the destination country's legal framework be monitored?
tia5_1.helpText: Describe how you will track legislative changes, court rulings, and enforcement actions (e.g., subscribe to EDPB/SA updates, legal counsel monitoring, vendor transparency reports).

tia5_2.text: What events should trigger a reassessment?
options: New legislation or executive order in the destination country
Data importer receives a government access request
Change in data categories or volume transferred
Change of sub-processor or onward transfer
Supervisory authority guidance or enforcement action

tia5_3.text: When is the next scheduled review?
tia5_3.helpText: Provide a specific date or timeframe (e.g., 'within 12 months' or '2027-04-01').

s1.title: Processing Description
s1.description: Describe the nature, scope, and purpose of the processing

s2.title: Scope & Context
s2.description: Assess the scope of processing and the context in which data subjects interact with you

s3.title: Necessity & Proportionality
s3.description: Demonstrate that the processing is necessary and proportionate to the purpose

s4.title: Consultation
s4.description: Document consultation with stakeholders, the DPO, and (where appropriate) data subjects

s5.title: Risk Identification
s5.description: Identify risks to the rights and freedoms of data subjects

s6.title: Mitigation Measures
s6.description: Document technical and organizational measures to mitigate identified risks

s7.title: Residual Risk & Conclusion
s7.description: Assess residual risk after mitigations and determine whether supervisory authority consultation is required

q1_1.text: What categories of personal data will be processed?
q1_1.helpText: List all data categories (e.g. identifiers, financial, behavioral, special category). Be specific about each category.

q1_2.text: What is the purpose of the processing?
q1_2.helpText: Describe the primary and any secondary purposes. Reference specific business objectives.

q1_3.text: What is the legal basis for processing?

q1_5.text: Estimated number of data subjects affected

q1_6.text: Describe the data flows and recipients
q1_6.helpText: Map how data moves through your systems — from collection to storage, processing, sharing, and deletion. Identify all recipients and processors.

q2_1.text: What is the sensitivity level of the data?
options: Mix of non-sensitive and sensitive indicators
Includes special category data (Art. 9) or criminal offence data (Art. 10)

q2_2.helpText: State retention periods for each data category and justify why that duration is necessary.

q2_3.helpText: Identify all countries where data is processed or stored, and the transfer mechanisms used (e.g. adequacy decision, SCCs, BCRs).

q2_4.text: Would data subjects reasonably expect this processing?
options: Fully expected — directly related to the service they signed up for
Probably expected — a reasonable extension of the service
Possibly unexpected — not directly related to the primary service
Unexpected — data subjects would likely be surprised

q3_1.text: Is the processing necessary to achieve the stated purpose?
options: Essential — cannot achieve the purpose without it
Highly beneficial — significantly more effective than alternatives
Somewhat beneficial — marginal improvement over alternatives
Not clearly necessary — alternatives could achieve the same result

q3_2.text: What alternatives were considered and why were they rejected?
q3_2.helpText: Describe less intrusive alternatives you evaluated and explain why the chosen approach is necessary.

q3_3.text: What data minimization measures are in place?
q3_3.helpText: Describe measures to ensure only necessary data is collected and processed (e.g. pseudonymization, aggregation, field-level restrictions).

q4_1.text: Who was consulted during this assessment?

q4_2.text: Summary of consultation outcomes and actions taken
q4_2.helpText: Summarize feedback received, objections raised, and any changes made to the processing as a result.

q5_1.text: Does the processing involve automated decision-making or profiling?

q5_2.text: Does the processing involve special category data or criminal offence data?

q5_3.text: Does the processing involve systematic monitoring of a publicly accessible area?

q5_4.text: Is the processing carried out on a large scale?

q5_5.text: Does the processing involve innovative use of technology (e.g. AI, biometrics, IoT)?

q5_6.text: Describe the identified risks to data subjects, including likelihood and severity for each
q5_6.helpText: For each risk: describe the source, affected rights, likelihood (Remote / Possible / Likely), and severity (Minimal / Significant / Severe / Critical). Use the format: Risk N — Title: Description. Likelihood: X. Severity: Y.

q6_1.text: Technical measures in place or planned

q6_2.text: Organizational measures in place or planned

q6_3.text: Safeguards for individual rights (transparency, consent mechanisms, portability, opt-out)
q6_3.helpText: Describe how data subject rights are facilitated — e.g. privacy notices, consent preference centers, data portability tools, opt-out mechanisms.

q7_1.text: Overall residual risk level after mitigations

q7_2.text: Is prior consultation with the supervisory authority required? (Art. 36)
options: No — residual risk has been sufficiently mitigated
Yes — high residual risk that cannot be sufficiently mitigated

q7_3.helpText: Record the DPO's formal advice: whether the processing may proceed, any conditions, and the recommended review date.
